tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read raw file contents. For code files: PREFER code_search(file_outline) to get structure, and code_search(get_source) to read specific symbols. Only use read_file for non-code files, configuration, or when you need raw implementation logic that code_search cannot provide. When using start_line/end_line, always read at least 100 lines per call.

path.description: File path relative to repo root

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates parent directories if needed. Use for generating documentation files.

path.description: File path relative to repo root

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List files and subdirectories at a path. Returns names with type indicators (file/dir).

path.description: Directory path relative to repo root (default: '.')

recursive.description: List recursively up to depth 3 (default: false)

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: Search for a regex pattern in files. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers.

path.description: Directory to search in (default: repo root)

tools.name: get_file_tree
tools.description: Get the full file tree of the repository as indented text. Respects .gitignore.

tools.name: get_db_state
tools.description: Query the Spark SQLite database for file states, documented files, or stale files.

tools.name: update_rules_index
tools.description: Add or update an entry in a RULES_INDEX.md file.

index_path.description: Path to RULES_INDEX.md relative to repo root

entry_path.description: Relative path to the rule file from the index

summary.description: One-line summary for the index entry

section.description: Section heading to place the entry under (e.g. 'Documentation Rules')

tools.name: ask_user
tools.description: Ask the user a question in the terminal. Returns their answer. Supports default values (user presses Enter to accept).

question.description: The question to display to the user

default.description: Default value shown in brackets, returned if user presses Enter

hint.description: Optional hint displayed before the question

tools.name: read_spark_plans
tools.description: Read all project plan .md files and spark_init.json from .claude/spark_plans/. Returns plan contents and init settings.

tools.name: install_templates
tools.description: Install Agent Blueprint templates into the target directory. Adapts to platform (claude/windsurf/copilot/codex). Can overwrite existing files if requested.

target_dir.description: Target directory to install into (default: current dir)

overwrite_rules.description: Overwrite existing rules and skills files (default: false)

tools.name: scan_existing
tools.description: Scan target repo for existing platform directories, instructions files, rules, and skills. Use before install_templates to decide what to overwrite.

target_dir.description: Target directory to scan (default: current dir)

tools.name: list_library
tools.description: List all plugins in the Spark library catalog. Optionally filter by category. Returns summaries (id, name, description, category, tags).

category.description: Filter by category (e.g. 'memory', 'testing'). Empty for all.

tools.name: search_library
tools.description: Search the Spark library catalog by keyword. Matches against name, description, tags, and category.

tools.name: get_plugin_details
tools.description: Get full details for a specific plugin including pros, cons, config options, and integration info.

tools.name: install_plugin
tools.description: Install a plugin from the Spark library. Clones the repo, copies rules/skills into the project, and updates RULES_INDEX.md.

config_overrides.description: JSON string of config option overrides (default: '{}')

tools.name: code_search
tools.description: PRIMARY tool for code exploration. Use INSTEAD of read_file for all code analysis. Key actions: file_outline (get all symbols in a file — use this first), get_source (read a specific function/class by symbol_id), dependency_graph (imports and importers), search (find symbols by name), call_hierarchy (callers/callees across files), impact_preview (what breaks if symbol changes), related_symbols (find functionally related symbols), hotspots (complexity × churn risk), repo_health (one-call triage), find_references (who imports this identifier), context_bundle (symbol + imports with token budgeting), ranked_context (BM25 + PageRank query search). Much faster and more accurate than reading raw files.

action.description: search: find symbols by query. get_source: get source for a symbol ID. file_outline: list symbols in a file. dependency_graph: file-level import graph. blast_radius: files affected by changing a symbol. symbol_complexity: cyclomatic complexity metrics. repo_overview: high-level repo outline. coupling_metrics: afferent/efferent coupling for a file. symbol_importance: rank symbols by PageRank centrality. changed_symbols: symbols changed since a git SHA. dependency_cycles: circular dependency detection. class_hierarchy: inheritance tree for a class. find_importers: files that import a given file. call_hierarchy: callers and callees for a symbol N levels deep (direction: callers/callees/both). impact_preview: transitive what-breaks analysis for a symbol. related_symbols: heuristic clustering of related symbols (same-file, shared-importers, name overlap). hotspots: complexity × churn scoring for risk detection. repo_health: one-call triage (dead code, complexity, hotspots, cycles, unstable modules). find_references: import-level identifier lookup with optional call-chain enrichment. context_bundle: symbol + imports bundle with token budgeting. ranked_context: BM25 + PageRank combined query search with greedy token packing.

symbol_id.description: Symbol ID like 'src/auth.py::login#function' (for get_source, blast_radius, symbol_complexity, call_hierarchy, impact_preview, related_symbols, context_bundle)

file_path.description: File path relative to repo root (for file_outline, dependency_graph, coupling_metrics, find_importers)

kind.description: Filter by symbol kind: function, class, method, variable, etc. (for search action)

file_pattern.description: Glob pattern to filter files, e.g. 'src/auth/**' (for search action)

limit.description: Max results (default 20, 0 for all). Also used as top_n for symbol_importance/hotspots and max_results for related_symbols/find_references.

direction.description: Direction of traversal. For dependency_graph: imports/importers/both. For call_hierarchy: callers/callees/both. Default varies by action.

depth.description: Traversal depth (1-3 for dependency_graph/blast_radius, 1-5 for call_hierarchy, default: 1)

since_sha.description: Git SHA to compare against for changed_symbols action

include_blast_radius.description: Include blast radius per changed symbol (for changed_symbols action)

scope.description: Glob pattern to scope symbol_importance/ranked_context analysis, e.g. 'src/**'

identifier.description: Symbol or module name to look up (for find_references, singular mode)

identifiers.description: JSON array of symbol/module names for batch find_references, e.g. '["Database", "Config"]'

include_call_chain.description: Enrich find_references results with calling_symbols in each file (default: false)

include_callers.description: Add caller file list to context_bundle results (default: false)

token_budget.description: Max tokens to return for context_bundle/ranked_context. 0 = no limit (default: 0 for context_bundle, 4000 for ranked_context)

budget_strategy.description: How to rank/trim when token_budget is set in context_bundle: most_relevant (by centrality), core_first (primary symbol first), compact (signatures only)

strategy.description: Ranking strategy for ranked_context: combined (BM25 + PageRank), bm25 (text relevance only), centrality (PageRank only)

include_kinds.description: JSON array of symbol kinds to restrict ranked_context results, e.g. '["function", "class"]'

days.description: Churn look-back window in days for hotspots/repo_health (default: 90)

min_complexity.description: Minimum cyclomatic complexity for hotspots (default: 2, filters trivial getters/setters)

tools.name: code_index
tools.description: Create or update the code symbol index for the repository. Also checks index status.

action.description: index: create/update the code index. status: check if index exists.
